title: Iceberg Conformance Expected Output (golden)
description: The expected result of running an op-log against an implementation. Reuses the read-side fixture vocabulary from sungwy/iceberg-testing#2 (accept / clause / decoded-rows keyed by field-id / type-annotated value tree) and extends it with a snapshots section for the DML (write) side, since an op-log mutates a table across multiple snapshots and observation points. A read-only spec (single observation, no mutations) collapses to exactly the PR #2 shape.

accept.description: PR #2 semantics: true if a conformant implementation runs the op-log and reads it back; false if the input is meant to be rejected.

clause.description: PR #2: the spec behavior this case pins

reason.description: PR #2: present when accept=false; the spec invariant the input violates

snapshots.description: DML-side extension (absent in a read-only PR #2 case). Commit-ordered snapshots produced by the op-log. Reuses PR #2's manifest/delete-file vocabulary.

ordinal.description: commit ordinal (0,1,2...); replaces the implementation's random snapshot-id

summary.description: Only layout-independent aggregate metrics (per-file metrics vary with file splitting and are excluded).

delete-files.description: Spec-pinned facts about delete files added in this snapshot, in PR #2's manifest vocabulary. No paths/sizes.

equality-ids.description: Present for content=2. PR #2 shape: element type retained (spec pins int) to catch iceberg-go#880.

observations.description: One entry per 'observe' in the op-log, in order. Each is a PR #2-style decoded read of the table at that point.

at.description: the resolved observation point: a bound name, 'latest', or a commit ordinal

iceberg-schema.description: PR #2 shape: field-id, name, type per top-level column at this observation (the schema may differ across observations under schema evolution).

decoded-rows.description: PR #2 shape: one entry per row, keyed by field-id, value as a type-annotated tree. Order is NOT significant (a scan is unordered); the orchestrator sorts by the __rowkey column before comparison. A physical/SQL-null row is a bare null.

valueNode.description: PR #2 type-annotated value tree. Primitive: {type,value}. Object: {type:object,fields}. Array: {type:array,elements}. Variant null: {type:null}. 64-bit integers are JSON strings.
